title: sedum album subsp. teretifolium
intro: Sedum album subsp. teretifolium, also known as narrow-leaved white stonecrop, is a subspecies of the Sedum album plant, characterized by its narrow, fleshy leaves and ability to thrive in dry, rocky environments. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Sedum album subsp. teretifolium include its potential to reduce fever, soothe wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Sedum album subsp. teretifolium can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations like infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
